finding: F04 cube-derived CFAR export imported into F03
Stage 08 Kalman tracking ran on F04 cube detections
Stage 12.5 sequence-prior scoring ran on F04 tracks
Stage 12.5 false-track suppression under cube-CFAR detections (windowable denominator)
interpretation: stage 12.5 removes only 26.8% of false tracks -- structured CFAR clutter is substantially harder than F02 point clutter (denominator: windowable scored tracks only)

finding: Stage 12.5 true-track retention under cube-CFAR detections
interpretation: true-track retention is only 88.6%; the filter is losing real targets

finding: Are cube-CFAR false tracks harder than F02 point-detection false tracks?
interpretation: F04 false_reduction 0.2680 vs F02 four-day pooled 0.9939 (delta -0.7258): cube-CFAR false tracks look harder. Different detection source, frame count and threshold semantics -- suggestive, not a controlled comparison

finding: CFAR threshold scale is not a dB SNR threshold
interpretation: thr_6p0dB is a compatibility filename token only; it represents F04 CFAR scale 6, not a 6 dB SNR threshold. CFAR scale 6 is 7.78 dB above the local noise estimate.

finding: Calibration provenance for the stage 12.5 run
interpretation: noise-matched calibration built from the F04 cube tracks themselves (not the canonical F02 tracks)

finding: Most stage-08 false tracks are never scored by stage 12.5
interpretation: stage 08 confirmed 20,457 false tracks but only 5,141 are windowable (>=1 window), so 15,316 are never seen by the filter. The 26.8% reduction is over the windowable subset only

finding: Pipeline-level false-track suppression over ALL stage-08 false tracks
interpretation: if unscored confirmed tracks pass through, stage 12.5 removes only 6.7% of the false tracks the tracker emitted. Dropping unscored tracks instead would raise suppression but collapse true-track retention -- stage 12.5 does not define this policy, so both are reported
